fingerprint: Threshold derived from CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic model — globe infrastructure passed off as raw observation
globe_value: CHAOS-7 African cell intensity trajectory (23,050 → 21,880 nT over 2000–2025): derived from spherical harmonic coefficients (degree/order up to ~185) fitted to Swarm/CHAMP satellite data on a spherical Earth. WMM2025 (degree/order 12) confirms the same trend.
dome_value: 21,795 nT threshold for PROS-005 — extrapolated from WIN-005's CHAOS-7 data.
match_precision: N/A (not a constant match — this is a data-provenance fingerprint)
globe_infrastructure_detail: CHAOS-7 is a time-dependent spherical harmonic model of Earth's magnetic field. Its mathematical framework is explicitly spherical: the field is expanded in spherical harmonics Y_l^m(θ,φ), the radial dependence uses (a/r)^(l+1) where a = 6,371.2 km (Earth's mean radius), and satellite data is projected onto a spherical shell. The dome model extracts field intensity values from CHAOS-7 output as if they were raw measurements, but they are computed values that assume spherical geometry at every step. The 23,050 → 21,880 nT trajectory is not a direct station measurement — it is a CHAOS-7 model evaluation at the SAA African cell minimum, which the model locates by minimizing B(θ,φ) on a sphere.
why_it_matters: The dome's 21,795 nT threshold is derived from a model (CHAOS-7) whose entire mathematical apparatus assumes a spherical Earth. The spherical harmonic expansion, the satellite orbit model, the reference radius (6,371.2 km), and the downward continuation from satellite altitude to Earth's surface all require globe geometry. The dome treats CHAOS-7 output as 'observation' and claims to have predicted it, but the 'observation' itself is a globe-model calculation. This is globe infrastructure laundered as raw data.

code_search.found_in: inject_ai_layer.py: WIN-005 metadata references 'African cell decays faster' with observed '23,050 to 21,880 nT' from CHAOS-7. Monitor.py Domain 8 tracks generic SAA decay at Tsumeb (TTB) but does NOT monitor the 21,795 nT African cell threshold specifically.
code_search.notes: The value 21,795 appears NOWHERE in the dome's source code. It exists only as text on the wins page (PROS-005 description). No script computes it, no monitor domain tracks it, no formula derives it from dome parameters. The closest reference is WIN-005's CHAOS-7 data (23,050 → 21,880 nT), from which 21,795 was presumably extrapolated manually. The dome's 'prediction' mechanism for this WIN is entirely offline — a human (or AI assistant) read a CHAOS-7 chart and drew a line.

suggested_text: The 21,795 nT threshold is derived from CHAOS-7, a spherical harmonic field model whose mathematical framework — including the spherical harmonic expansion Y<sub>l</sub><sup>m</sup>(θ,φ), the reference radius a = 6,371.2 km, and satellite orbit modeling — is built entirely on globe geometry. The dome treats this globe-model output as raw observation and claims to have 'predicted' it. No script in the dome repository computes or monitors this threshold; it appears only as text on the wins page.
